steps.title: Spot the location shift
steps.content: The passage presents one example at the University of Texas, then immediately mentions Howard University — a different school in a different city. The transition must signal movement to another location while continuing the same series of examples.

steps.title: Apply the location signal
steps.content:
The logical relationship is addition via location shift: two parallel examples of African American history courses at different institutions.
  • Choice A — "In other words,": signals restatement or clarification. The Howard example is not a rephrasing of the UT example. ✗
  • Choice B — "Elsewhere,": signals a shift to another location while maintaining the same illustrative function — exactly right for moving from UT to Howard. ✓
  • Choice C — "Thus,": signals a conclusion drawn from prior evidence. The Howard example is not a conclusion. ✗
  • Choice D — "In particular,": signals a narrowing specification. Howard is not a subset of UT; it is a separate institution. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
